scoring: Sum 0-9 per vertex. Highest sum is the weakest vertex. Tie-break: ask which weakness the user recognizes most viscerally.

questions.text: People in the group take on whatever's needed, regardless of role.
Disagreements tend to collapse into shared positions before the actual difference gets examined.
If someone asked each member 'what is your specific contribution that no one else makes?' — most would struggle to answer.
Recent meetings have produced presence and process, but not actual decisions or real exchange.
Communication is happening on schedule, but you couldn't point to what changed in anyone because of it.
There is proximity — shared channels, calls, space — but the substantive conversations happen elsewhere, or not at all.
When capacity runs out, the response is to push harder rather than name the limit.
Bad news gets softened, delayed, or hidden, because surfacing it feels like failure rather than information.
Someone — possibly you — is consistently performing beyond their actual capacity, and the system has adapted to their over-performance.
The same problems resurface week after week — nothing accumulates that prevents their return.
If the most central person stepped away for a month, the group's capacity would collapse rather than redistribute.
When surplus energy appears, it gets absorbed by maintenance rather than building something that didn't exist before.
